seo.title: Responsible AI Governance for Agents in Flowise Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix responsible ai governance for agents in Flowise agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Flowise, Responsible AI Governance for Agents fails when drag-and-drop chatflows produce unvalidated outputs, retrieve irrelevant documents, or lose context during multi-agent handoffs. These issues manifest as hallucinated responses, biased retrievals, and unsafe tool executions that bypass human oversight. Without governance, Flowise agents amplify risks across RAG pipelines and sequential agent workflows.

howItManifests.scenario: Chatflow chains producing unvalidated intermediate outputs
howItManifests.symptom: LLM Chain nodes output raw JSON without PII redaction or toxicity filtering
howItManifests.impact: Sensitive data exposure and harmful content propagation through workflow

howItManifests.scenario: Vector store retrieval returning low-relevance documents
howItManifests.symptom: Document Retriever nodes return biased/off-topic chunks without relevance scoring
howItManifests.impact: Hallucinations from poor context and fairness violations in retrieval

howItManifests.scenario: Custom tool nodes failing silently without error propagation
howItManifests.symptom: Tool nodes execute without input validation or safety guardrails
howItManifests.impact: Malicious inputs trigger unsafe API calls or data exfiltration

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-agent sequential flow losing context between agent handoffs
howItManifests.symptom: Agent handoff nodes drop governance metadata between sequential agents
howItManifests.impact: Cumulative drift leads to policy violations and untraceable decisions

detection.name: Unvalidated Chatflow Output Monitor
detection.description: Detects missing PII masking and toxicity scores in LLM Chain outputs

detection.name: Vector Retrieval Relevance Auditor
detection.description: Monitors Document Retriever nodes for low cosine similarity thresholds

detection.name: Tool Execution Safety Scanner
detection.description: Validates tool inputs for jailbreak attempts and dangerous patterns

fixes.name: PII Redaction Middleware
fixes.description: Automatically masks PII before outputs leave LLM Chain nodes

fixes.name: Relevance-Gated Retriever
fixes.description: Filters low-relevance documents before RAG synthesis

fixes.name: Context-Preserving Agent Handoff
fixes.description: Maintains governance metadata across multi-agent flows

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Responsible AI Governance for Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a Flowise callback handler to automatically detect governance violations across chatflows, retrievers, and agent handoffs. It monitors intermediate outputs for PII leakage, toxicity, relevance drift, and tool safety in real-time, blocking unsafe executions before they propagate. Vex's agent reliability engine maintains full audit trails and auto-remediates by injecting safety filters and context preservation.

The Vex SDK hooks into Flowise's node execution lifecycle, providing zero-code governance for drag-and-drop workflows. Teams get instant dashboards showing violation rates, risk trends, and automated fix recommendations. Vex scales across multi-agent flows while preserving Flowise's low-code simplicity.

Configuration takes 3 lines: install the SDK, add the callback handler, and define policies. Vex then handles detection, blocking, remediation, and reporting automatically.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add Vex to your Flowise chatflows in 3 lines and eliminate governance violations instantly.
